burden.dalyPerCapita: low mortality; significant QOL and productivity impact

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV criteria for IBS-D; exclude alarm features and inflammatory bowel disease

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; limited access to dietitian counseling

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Clinical diagnosis in primary care and gastroenterology
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Dietary modification, antispasmodics, loperamide, rifaximin for selected cases; psychological therapy when available
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Underdiagnosis and limited access to low FODMAP education
